task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly calculates the median of a list and handles edge cases properly.
trace: To calculate the median, the function first checks if the input list `nums` is empty and returns None if true. If not, it sorts the list. Depending on whether the length of the sorted list is odd or even, it calculates the median accordingly. For an odd number of elements, it returns the middle element; for an even number, it calculates the average of the two middle elements. This approach ensures that the function handles both cases correctly and efficiently.
